title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bondell discusses the death of Gerry Wade with Lord Cuttle, learning that Lanlan Wade is not Gerry's biological sister. Although she shares the Wade surname, she is the child of the old Wade's second wife, who took the name after the old Wade insisted upon it. Bondell realizes that Gerry's letter to Lanlan was unusual and decides she must find Jimmy Sassiage and speak with Lorraine Wade, believing Lorraine may hold clues regarding a seven-faced clock and an ominous message Gerry left her.

The next morning, Bondell calls Bill to ask about Jimmy Sassiage. Bill describes Jimmy as a smart but idle man with a rosy face. During the call, Bill mentions Ronnie Devereux, Gerry's best friend, noting that he hasn't seen him since the weekend Gerry died. Bondell realizes Bill is unaware of Ronnie's death and suspects the police may have covered up the news since it was not reported in the papers. She decides not to tell Bill the truth over the phone but agrees to meet him for dinner the following night.

Bill provides Jimmy's address as 103 Jemming Street. Bondell travels there and is met by a polite, expressionless man who leads her to a living room. There, she encounters a petite girl in a black dress. Recognizing the girl's mourning clothes, Bondell asks if she is Lorraine Wade. Lorraine confirms her identity and mentions receiving a letter from Bondell regarding Gerry. When Bondell asks about Ronnie Devereux, Lorraine reveals she knows he is dead. After Bondell recounts the previous day's events, a terrified Lorraine concludes that Gerry Wade did not die naturally but was murdered.
